title: Sphaeropteris macarenensis
taxonomy: Sphaeropteris macarenensis is a species of fern in the family Cyathea, commonly known as the Macarena tree fern. This species is native to the Macarena mountain range in Colombia. The classification of Sphaeropteris macarenensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Cyatheales, Family: Cyatheaceae, Genus: Sphaeropteris, Species: S. macarenensis. This species is also sometimes classified under the genus Cyathea, which is now widely considered to be a synonym of Sphaeropteris. Further research is needed to confirm its classification.

intro: Sphaeropteris macarenensis, also known as the Macarena Tree Fern, is a species of tree fern native to the tropical forests of South America, characterized by its distinctive rounded crown and robust fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeropteris macarenensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal activities. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are utilized to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to treat skin conditions and wounds.
